name: 5 Star Car Title Loans
description_short: Licensed title loan lender operating since 2012 with 255K+ loans funded. Borrow $100–$50K against your vehicle title while keeping your car; same-day funding available.
description_long:
5 Star Car Title Loans™ is a licensed and regulated consumer lending company headquartered in San Bernardino, California. Founded in 2012, the company has grown to serve customers across approximately 18 states including California, Florida, Texas, Michigan, and others, funding over 255,000 loans in its operating history. The company operates both online and through physical branch locations, making it accessible to a broad range of borrowers in the states where it is licensed.

The company's primary product is the auto title loan — a secured short-term loan where the borrower's vehicle title serves as collateral. Loan amounts range from $100 to $50,000, with the amount tied to the vehicle's assessed equity. Borrowers retain possession of their vehicle throughout the loan term. Beyond standard car title loans, 5 Star also offers installment loans with fixed monthly payments and repayment periods up to 24 months, same-day loans for urgent needs, and accepts non-standard collateral including motorcycles and semi-trucks. Applications can be submitted online or in person.

Several features distinguish 5 Star from competitors in the title loan space. The company advertises no prepayment penalties, transparent upfront terms with no hidden fees, and a streamlined three-step process that can result in same-day funding. The lender explicitly markets to borrowers with imperfect credit, positioning vehicle equity — rather than credit score — as the primary qualification factor. Its long operating history and 255K+ funded loans suggest stable operations, and it holds licenses across the states it serves.

Borrowers should approach title loans with caution regardless of lender. These products typically carry APRs well above 100%, which 5 Star does not disclose publicly on its website. Missing payments risks vehicle repossession, which can compound financial hardship. The company's on-site review base of 11 reviews is thin for a lender claiming 14 years of operation and 255K+ loans, limiting independent verification of customer experience. Title loans are best used as a last resort when no lower-cost option is available and the borrower has high confidence in their ability to repay within the loan term.
address: 4865 S 12th Ave suite a, Tucson, AZ 85714

company_info.headquarters: 4865 S 12th Ave suite a, Tucson, AZ 85714

pros: 255,000+ loans funded since 2012 — substantial track record in the title loan industry
Same-day funding available for approved applicants
Borrower keeps and drives their vehicle throughout the loan term
No prepayment penalties — pay off early without fees
Accepts motorcycles and semi-trucks as collateral, not just passenger vehicles
Licensed and regulated lender operating in ~18 states
Free pre-qualification available with no commitment required
cons: APR not disclosed on website — title loans typically carry triple-digit interest rates
Vehicle repossession risk if loan payments are missed
Loan availability and terms vary significantly by state — not all products available everywhere
Only 11 reviews displayed on their own website for a lender claiming 14 years of operation
Loan ceiling of $50,000 is tied to vehicle equity — owners of older or low-value vehicles may qualify for very little
best_for: Vehicle owners with equity who need emergency cash within 24 hours
Borrowers with poor or no credit who cannot qualify for personal loans or credit cards
Those facing urgent one-time expenses (medical bills, rent, car repairs) with a clear repayment plan
Commercial vehicle owners (semi-truck operators) needing collateral-based financing
diagnosis: 5 Star Car Title Loans is best suited for vehicle owners with usable equity who face an urgent cash need and cannot qualify for lower-cost alternatives. The main caveat is structural to the product category: title loans carry high APRs and put your vehicle at risk of repossession — borrowers should treat this as a last resort and have a concrete repayment plan before applying.
services: Installment loans (fixed payments, up to 24 months)
